page: Amazon Web Services c8i.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 16 vCPUs (8 physical cores with hyper-threading) running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The CPU architecture is x86_64, supported by a large 480 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the c8i.4xlarge demonstrates strong single-core capabilities. It achieves a top-tier Geekbench single-core score of 2198.0 and a text processing score of 2975.0, both placing in the top 10% of tested servers. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks also perform strongly, with gzip compression reaching 34.45 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.45 GB/sec. For machine learning workloads, the instance achieves a top-tier text generation speed of 348.03 tokens/sec using a small 135M LLM model, while multi-core operations, database tasks, and medium 7B LLM inference perform in the average range.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c8i.4xlarge offers a high-density compute profile without the added expense of bundled local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators. The inclusion of advanced instruction sets, such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512, enhances its value for vector and matrix math operations without requiring specialized hardware. This instance is well-suited for compute-bound workloads, including single-threaded processing tasks, software compilation, text processing, web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs (8 physical cores), a 480 MB L3 cache, and 32.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark results highlight top-tier single-core performance, particularly in Geekbench single-core and text processing tests, alongside strong single-threaded compression speeds. It also excels at lightweight LLM text generation, reaching 348.03 tokens/sec with a 135M model. The c8i.4xlarge is designed for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, text processing, and web serving where high-frequency single-core execution and advanced vector instructions are required.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and 8 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier single-core and text processing performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier single-core performance.
tagline: A high-frequency, compute-optimized x86_64 platform featuring advanced Intel matrix extensions and top-tier single-core processing capabilities.

32.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory (2.0 GB per core)
